memo_title: Macro Memo: Running Hot — AI Productivity, Geopolitics, New Macro Trades

active_trades.thesis_summary: Bitcoin is staging a catch-up rebound in Q1 2026; negative reflexivity from MSTR and ETF flows has abated and BTC is expected to trade back above 100K with potential retest of all-time highs.
active_trades.entry_notes: Exited Long IBIT/Short QQQ on January 5th, 2026 for ~8% gain and flipped long IBIT.

active_trades.thesis_summary: Oil had multiple negative catalysts and still closed up, positioning is very crowded on the short side, playing for mean reversion; geopolitical risk premium from Iran/Hormuz Strait provides additional upside.
active_trades.entry_notes: Went long WTI Crude Oil (CLH6) at $59.15 on January 12th, 2026; stop at entry given subsequent price action providing buffer.

active_trades.thesis_summary: December 2027 natural gas has pulled back from above 5.2 to 4.42, presenting a mean-reversion opportunity after having taken profits near the peak on the prior NGZ5 position.
active_trades.entry_notes: Looking to get long NGZ27 at current levels around 4.42 after rolling out from NGZ5 position.

active_trades.thesis_summary: Copper driven by LatAm supply chain realignment, US manufacturing onshoring demand, and AI/data center energy infrastructure buildout; structural demand tailwinds remain intact.
active_trades.entry_notes: Position rolled out to HGZ6 in prior memo; up ~20% since then with little sign of letting up.

active_trades.thesis_summary: Taking a contrarian 2s30s flattener as the steepener trade is very crowded; Powell could remain as Governor post-chair term limiting the new appointee's ability to ease, and the economy does not need more cuts.
active_trades.entry_notes: New trade initiated around memo date; trade carries well and is considered the pain trade given consensus is positioned for steepening.

active_trades.thesis_summary: Tankers benefit from multiple independent catalysts: record Iranian crude on tankers, aging global fleet, tightening Western sanctions on shadow shipping, and VLCC market at multi-year highs; asymmetrically convex to Iran/US tensions escalating.
active_trades.entry_notes: New trade initiated around memo date; basket available on the Citrini portal.

active_trades.thesis_summary: China corporate sector recovery driven by anti-involution push, improving price levels, recovering consumer demand, and continued Beijing investment in advanced technology; trade tensions expected to ease ahead of US midterms.
active_trades.entry_notes: Long FXI March 20-delta calls; among favorite ex-US equity index picks.

active_trades.thesis_summary: Malaysia is a clean play on ASEAN onshoring in electronics and Southeast Asia's data-center buildout with supportive macro, currency valuation cushion, and equity index tilted toward early-cycle beneficiaries.
active_trades.entry_notes: Among favorite ex-US equity index picks listed as new trades.

active_trades.thesis_summary: Chile ETF provides exposure to copper and lithium miners benefiting from US supply chain realignment toward LatAm, pro-market political shift under Jose Antonio Kast, and diminishing political risk premium.
active_trades.entry_notes: Among favorite ex-US equity index picks listed as new trades.

active_trades.thesis_summary: Rocket Mortgage is a direct beneficiary of the Trump administration's push to lower mortgage rates ahead of midterms via MBS purchases and GSE cap removal; company also owns Redfin and Mr. Cooper.
active_trades.entry_notes: Previously initiated; thesis reinforced by strong new home sales data and continued policy focus on housing affordability.

watchlist_themes.theme: Housing affordability / mortgage rate decline beneficiaries
watchlist_themes.rationale: Trump administration is aggressively targeting lower mortgage rates ahead of midterms via MBS purchases, which should unlock existing home inventory and benefit moving, home improvement, and luxury home furnishing names.

US supply chain realignment toward LatAm under the Don-roe Doctrine is structurally bullish for Chilean and broader LatAm copper and lithium producers as the US secures critical mineral supply chains.

Vietnam, Indonesia, Malaysia, and Philippines all have equity risk premiums that still price chronic policy and inflation risk even as those risks have become tailwinds, with multiple forced-buyer catalysts including index inclusion and capex cycles.

Chinese capital markets are heating up in favored industries like semiconductors and robotics, with potential Nvidia rivals debuting at multi-bagger valuations and Beijing continuing advanced technology investment.

Trump's push to make tech companies pay for their own data center power could benefit both behind-the-meter generators like Bloom Energy and regulated utilities as a lower-risk way to play the DC power narrative.

watchlist_themes.theme: Korea and Japan fiscal and supply chain plays
watchlist_themes.rationale: Korea is the largest holding in the Citrini Fiscal basket as a critical US ally in supply chain redesign; Japan benefits from fiscal tailwinds of the Takaichi Trade and strong robotics and semiconductor sector exposure.

Venezuelan bonds have already rallied 37% post-Maduro capture; positive spillovers from US-aligned political changes in LatAm could extend to financials and consumer discretionary in Argentina, Chile, and Brazil.

watchlist_themes.theme: XLU/SPY relative value on market weakness
watchlist_themes.rationale: Long XLU/short SPY is flagged as a go-to defensive trade for any broad market weakness, kept on the radar despite being stopped out previously due to a head-fake.

macro_view.rates_view: Rates are too low given the economic picture; strong nominal GDP growth driven by AI productivity should cause markets to remove the two Fed cuts priced in by end of 2026, with the 2-year at 3.5% likely to move higher; the 2s30s curve is expected to flatten as the steepener trade unwinds.
macro_view.dollar_view: bullish — removal of priced-in rate cuts driven by above-trend nominal GDP growth and AI productivity gains is expected to support a stronger dollar.
key_risks: Diplomatic resolution with Iran removing oil and tanker geopolitical risk premium
Federal Reserve / Trump political conflict escalating and disrupting monetary policy transmission
GDP growth overstated and subject to downward revision, with labor market weakness eventually prevailing
AI productivity gains prove transitory or are written off, undermining the core growth thesis
Tariff re-escalation with China disrupting LatAm supply chain realignment and SEA trade flows
